conditions: Only the closest piling at ~4 ft is clear with visible structure; the 11 ft piling is barely discernible as a hazy silhouette and farther pilings are not visible at all. This corresponds to very low visibility, dominated by turbidity and backscatter near the camera.

Closest 4ft and 11ft pilings are very clear with good texture. The 14ft/back-left piling is visible but somewhat hazy; farther 30ft pilings are not distinctly detectable. This corresponds best to the 14ft piling being hazy/faded, matching ~15 ft guideline.

Closest 4ft piling at right edge is clearly visible but somewhat hazy; the 11ft piling is only barely discernible as a faint silhouette and farther pilings are obscured. This corresponds to visibility where only the nearest piling is really clear.
Only the nearest 4 ft piling on the right edge is faintly visible; all more distant pilings are lost in green haze, indicating extremely poor water clarity (<5 ft).

Closest 4 ft piling on right is very sharp with clear texture. The 11 ft piling (just left of it) is also clearly visible but with slightly reduced detail. The 14 ft and 30 ft pilings toward the center/left are only vague dark shapes with almost no structural detail. This matches conditions where only the near pilings are clear and more distant ones are hazy silhouettes, corresponding to about 10 ft visibility.

The nearest 4 ft piling at right is sharp with clear texture. The mid/right (~11 ft) piling is visible but hazy. The 14 ft plus farther pilings are only faint silhouettes with little detail. This corresponds best to conditions where the 14 ft piling is hazy/faded, giving about 15 ft of visibility.
